channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian Government Rules Out Reopening Closed Oil Refineries
item.description: Australia’s Climate and Energy Minister Chris Bowen has confirmed that the government has no plans to reopen the country’s four oil refineries that shut down between 2013 and 2022. Speaking at a press...

item.title: World Leaders Condemn Political Violence Following Shooting at Trump Event
item.description: Leaders around the world have strongly condemned the shooting incident at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, expressing relief that US President Donald Trump and other attendees wer...

item.title: Court of Appeal Confirms Gabam as SDP Chairman as INEC Updates Party Leadership
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) don officially recognise the National Working Committee (NWC) of the Social Democratic Party (SDP) wey Shehu Musa Gabam dey lead, putting an end to...

item.title: NEET UG 2026 Admit Card Release and Exam Pattern Information
item.description: The NEET UG 2026 admit card is set to be released today for candidates who have registered for the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET). The admit card will be available for download on the o...

item.title: ANC moves to remove Madibeng mayor amid bribery allegations and internal dispute
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC) has taken the unusual step of tabling a motion of no confidence against Madibeng mayor Douglas Maimane after he refused to step down despite being formally recalled...

item.title: Anthropic tests AI-to-AI marketplace where agents negotiate real purchases
item.description: Anthropic recently conducted an internal experiment called Project Deal to explore how AI agents perform in a marketplace setting where they represent human users. In this pilot, 69 Anthropic employee...

item.title: Manitoba government plans restrictions on children’s access to social media and AI chatbots
item.description: The Manitoba government is preparing to introduce measures that would restrict children from accessing social media platforms and artificial intelligence chatbots, according to Premier Wab Kinew. The ...

item.title: Park Bom shares her admiration for Lee Min-ho once more
item.description: South Korean singer Park Bom recently expressed her admiration for actor Lee Min-ho, showing once again the affection she holds for the famed star. In various public statements and through social medi...

item.title: Commission on Human Rights Investigates Incident in Negros Occidental
item.description: The Commission on Human Rights (CHR) has announced an independent investigation into a recent clash that occurred in Negros Occidental. Details surrounding the incident remain somewhat scarce, but the...

item.title: Charlton Athletic Maintain Championship Status Despite Sichenje Injury
item.description: Charlton Athletic have managed to retain their place in the Championship for the next season after a crucial 2-1 victory against Hull City on Saturday. It was a real hustle for them, defying expectati...

item.title: Auto sector faces rising imports as new entrants lag in localisation commitments
item.description: Pakistan’s auto industry is facing growing concern as new car assemblers appear unwilling to disclose or commit to meaningful localisation of parts. Industry stakeholders, especially local vendors, ar...

item.title: Public, unions criticise fresh fuel price hike amid rising inflation concerns
item.description: The recent increase in petroleum product prices in Pakistan has triggered strong public reaction, with citizens, trade unions, and civil society groups expressing serious concern over the growing fina...

item.title: Borno APC leaders declare support for Tinubu and Shettima second-term bid
item.description: Leaders of the All Progressives Congress (APC) for Borno State don openly show strong support for President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and Vice-President Kashim Shettima to run again for second term ahead of t...

item.title: How AI is Impacting Human Intelligence and What We Can Do About It
item.description: Vivienne Ming, a theoretical neuroscientist and cognitive scientist, highlights the growing concern in the AI industry about whether AI is enhancing or undermining human intelligence. In an experiment...

item.title: Gen Z's Embrace of Officewear and Workwear: A Fashion Trend Explained
item.description: A fascinating shift is occurring in Canberra and beyond, as Gen Z embraces aesthetics inspired by office attire and traditional workwear. It's no longer just about the clothes; it’s about projecting a...

item.title: Trump Unfazed by White House Shooting, Remains Committed to Iran Conflict
item.description: The United States President Donald Trump, in a rather unexpected turn of events, declared that a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner wouldn't change his stance on t...

item.title: State attorney flags fraud and collusion within legal briefing system
item.description: South Africa’s Office of the State Attorney (OSA) has raised serious concerns about corruption, fraud, and collusion within its own ranks and among private legal practitioners it briefs. Acting solici...

item.title: Trump and White House Officials Evacuated After Shooting at Washington Dinner
item.description: President Donald Trump was unharmed, and other top White House officials were evacuated from an annual dinner of the White House Correspondents' Association after a man armed with guns and knives stor...

item.title: Broadway Revival of August Wilson's 'Joe Turner's Come and Gone' Struggles to Find its Rhythm
item.description: Debbie Allen’s revival of August Wilson’s seminal play, 'Joe Turner’s Come and Gone,' has recently graced the Ethel Barrymore Theatre on Broadway, marking its third staging since the 1984 premiere. Wh...

item.title: Carolina Hurricanes sweep Ottawa Senators in first-round playoff series
item.description: The Carolina Hurricanes have eliminated the Ottawa Senators from the Stanley Cup playoffs, winning the first-round series 4-0. In a decisive 4-2 victory, Logan Stankoven scored the go-ahead goal in th...
